FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Class DragDropContext

Namespace
FluentGpu.Input
Assembly
FluentGpu.Input.dll

L2 of the drag-drop stack (plan E5; user ruling 2026-06-10: deliberately NOT WinUI's OLE DataPackage/DoDragDrop modal loop — the Flutter Draggable/DragTarget + react-beautiful-dnd context + SwiftUI draggable/dropDestination model instead; the frame loop STAYS LIVE for the whole gesture). Layered on the L1 DragController gesture:

• A BoxEl.Draggable (DragSource: a string Kind discriminator + a payload factory) marks a node a typed drag source. When the L1 press promotes past the drag box, the dispatcher calls TryBegin(NodeHandle, Point2, KeyModifiers, PointerKind) — the nearest enabled DragSource up the chain opens THE session and its PayloadFactory resolves ONCE (cold gesture edge; never per-move). • A BoxEl.DropTarget (DropTargetSpec) makes ANY surface a candidate receiver. Per pointer move the dispatcher hands Move(NodeHandle, Point2, float, float, KeyModifiers) the hit-test chain under the pointer; the nearest enabled target whose AcceptKinds contains the session's Kind becomes OverTarget (a non-accepting target never blocks an accepting ancestor — the Flutter DragTarget pass-through). The dragged subtree can never self-target: L1 already cleared its HitTestVisible, so hit-testing sees THROUGH the moving visual. • Enter/Leave fire on target transitions, Over on every move while inside, Drop on release over an accepting target (TryDrop(Point2, KeyModifiers, out bool) — fired BEFORE the L1 completion so the target reads the live session). The ONE mutable DragSession instance is reused for the whole gesture (0 steady-state alloc per move); handlers copy what they keep.

Edge auto-scroll (the rbd/WinUI "drag near the viewport edge keeps scrolling" behavior) is engine-level here so every drag gets it: while a session is live, the pointer entering the hot zone of the nearest OVERFLOWING scrollable ancestor arms a proportional scroll velocity the host drives via Tick(float). Constants and behavior are WinUI's (microsoft-ui-xaml dxaml\xcp\dxaml\lib\ListViewBase_Partial_Reorder.cpp): a 100px edge band (:39 LISTVIEWBASE_EDGE_SCROLL_EDGE_WIDTH_PX), speed varying LINEARLY from 1500 px/s at the edge to 150 px/s at the band's inner rim (:42-47), a 50ms delay-start with instant velocity updates once running (:40, :1749-1753), the start-edge tried before the end-edge (:1660-1707), and scrolling suppressed when already pinned to that boundary (:1686-1690, :1718-1722).

public sealed class DragDropContext

Constructors

DragDropContext(SceneStore, Action) 

public DragDropContext(SceneStore scene, Action requestRerender)

Parameters

scene SceneStore
requestRerender Action

Fields

EdgeScrollMaxSpeed

Speed AT the edge — LISTVIEWBASE_EDGE_SCROLL_MAX_SPEED = 1500 px/s (:47).

public const float EdgeScrollMaxSpeed = 1500

Field Value

float

EdgeScrollMinSpeed

Speed at the inner rim of the zone — LISTVIEWBASE_EDGE_SCROLL_MIN_SPEED = 150 px/s (:46).

public const float EdgeScrollMinSpeed = 150

Field Value

float

EdgeScrollStartDelayMs

Delay before an armed edge scroll starts moving — LISTVIEWBASE_EDGE_SCROLL_START_DELAY_MSEC = 50 (:40). Velocity CHANGES while running apply instantly (:1749-1753).

public const float EdgeScrollStartDelayMs = 50

Field Value

float

EdgeScrollZonePx

Edge hot-zone width — LISTVIEWBASE_EDGE_SCROLL_EDGE_WIDTH_PX = 100 (ListViewBase_Partial_Reorder.cpp:39).

public const float EdgeScrollZonePx = 100

Field Value

float

Properties

HasActiveWork

True while an edge auto-scroll is armed or running — the host keeps frames coming and calls Tick(float).

public bool HasActiveWork { get; }

Property Value

bool

IsActive

A typed session is live (an L1 drag promoted on a chain carrying a DragSource).

public bool IsActive { get; }

Property Value

bool

OverTarget

The accepting target currently under the pointer (Null when none / idle).

public NodeHandle OverTarget { get; }

Property Value

NodeHandle

Session

THE live session object (meaningful only while IsActive). One instance, reused.

public DragSession Session { get; }

Property Value

DragSession

Methods

Cancel()

Abort the session (Escape / capture loss / window blur / release over nothing accepting follows TryDrop(Point2, KeyModifiers, out bool) returning false): fires OnLeave on a live current target, then resets. The L1 spring-back (the dispatcher's subsequent Cancel()/Complete) glides the visual home. No-op when idle.

public void Cancel()

Move(NodeHandle, Point2, float, float, KeyModifiers)

Per pointer move while the session is live: update the session coords/velocity, resolve the nearest accepting target on hit's parent chain, fire Enter/Leave on transitions and Over while inside, then re-evaluate the edge auto-scroll. 0-alloc.

public void Move(NodeHandle hit, Point2 abs, float velocityX, float velocityY, KeyModifiers mods)

Parameters

hit NodeHandle
abs Point2
velocityX float
velocityY float
mods KeyModifiers

PruneDead()

Called at dispatch start: a session whose SOURCE was freed by a reconcile ends (Leave fires on a live target); a freed TARGET/viewport is dropped silently (its columns are dead).

public void PruneDead()

Tick(float)

Phase-7 host tick: drive the armed edge auto-scroll — hold the 50ms delay-start, then scroll the viewport by velocity·dt through FluentGpu.Input.DragDropContext.ScrollBy (clamped writes; hitting the boundary stops it, the WinUI at-the-edge suppression). Returns true while armed/scrolling so the host keeps frames coming. 0-alloc.

public bool Tick(float dtMs)

Parameters

dtMs float

Returns

bool

TryBegin(NodeHandle, Point2, KeyModifiers, PointerKind)

Called by the dispatcher at L1 promotion: walk up from the promoted node for the nearest ENABLED DragSource, resolve its payload once, and open the session. Returns false when the chain carries no source — the gesture stays a plain L1 reorder drag and drop targets never see it.

public bool TryBegin(NodeHandle promoted, Point2 abs, KeyModifiers mods, PointerKind kind)

Parameters

promoted NodeHandle
abs Point2
mods KeyModifiers
kind PointerKind

Returns

bool

TryDrop(Point2, KeyModifiers, out bool)

Release: when the pointer is over an accepting target, fire OnDrop(session) and close the session — called by the dispatcher BEFORE the L1 Complete(Point2, KeyModifiers, uint, bool) so the target reads the live session. Returns true on a drop; settleGlide = the target's SettleOnDrop (true ⇒ keep the L1 drop-settle glide — reorder targets; false ⇒ the visual snaps home, the "deposited" feel of a foreign-surface drop).

public bool TryDrop(Point2 abs, KeyModifiers mods, out bool settleGlide)

Parameters

abs Point2
mods KeyModifiers
settleGlide bool

Returns

bool
FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
